setting.note: A single Wednesday in mid-June 1923. The novel is ordered by the striking of clocks.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Woolf died 1941; PD in the UK and EU (life+70, since 2012) and in the US (published 1925, PD since 2021).
rights.textSource: Standard Ebooks (public domain dedication), from a Project Gutenberg Australia transcription and Internet Archive scans
rights.territoryNotes: Verify locally: PD in life+70 territories since 2012, US since 2021. Not yet confirmed PD in life+80 or longer jurisdictions.
rights.attribution: Text: Virginia Woolf, Mrs Dalloway (1925). Transcription: Standard Ebooks.

palette.accentSourceQuote: laid her green dress on her bed
palette.note: The accent is a quotation, not a brand choice. Every book's colour must be citable in its own text, and the build checks that the quote exists — see the schema. Crime and Punishment will be Petersburg yellow for the same reason.

clarissa.name: Clarissa Dalloway
clarissa.colorSourceQuote: laid her green dress on her bed

historical.name: Ordnance Survey, London, 1890s
historical.attribution: Historical map: National Library of Scotland (CC BY-NC-SA)
historical.note: OS London Town Plan, 1890s. Thirty years before the novel, but the closest large-scale free layer; the street fabric Woolf walked is substantially this one.
